summary: Hezbollah operators using fiber-optic FPV drones struck two Israeli Namer MBTs and a HMMWV armored vehicle, with the latter catching fire. Russian "Rubicon" drone operators achieved a record 3,170 strikes in March 2026, a 40% increase over November 2025 and 5.7 times more than the same period in 2025, with over 1,500 strikes on exposed enemy infantry in the past six months. On March 31, 2026, Rubicon operators conducted strikes in Donbass, Krasny Liman, and Sumy directions, destroying Ukrainian personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, BM-21 Grad MLRS, and various vehicles and armored targets. Ukrainian forces used drone detectors to survive an FPV attack in the Kostiantinivka direction, while Russian drones eliminated a Ka-52 crew after it crash-landed near Nadiivka, striking during the evacuation attempt.
